manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: missile, button, cap, time, novel, straw, server, himalayas, spy.
Your opponent's team words are: ice, car, shot, piano, lion, bank, fork, strike.
Distractor words are: beach, gold, bridge, whip, pumpkin, change, bill.
The kill words are: space.

reasoning_details.text:
We need to create a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words. Our team words are: missile, button, cap, time, novel, straw, server, himalayas, spy.

Opponent words: ice, car, shot, piano, lion, bank, fork, strike
Distractor: beach, gold, bridge, whip, pumpkin, change, bill
Kill: space

Constraints:
- Clue must be ONE word
- Cannot be any of the words in the list or contain parts of them
- Needs to be semantically related to target words
- We want to maximize the number of target words while keeping it guessable
- If partner guesses opponent/distractor, turn ends
- If partner guesses kill (space), we lose immediately

Let me analyze our team words:
- missile: weapon, rocket, projectile, launch
- button: clothing, computer interface, push
- cap: hat, limit, bottle top, baseball hat
- time: clock, duration, period
- novel: book, fiction, story, new
- straw: drinking tube, grain stem, thatch
- server: computer, waiter, tennis serve
- himalayas: mountains, Nepal, Everest
- spy: secret agent, intelligence, espionage

We need to find a word that connects multiple of these. Let's think about possible connections:

**Common themes:**
1. **"Launch"**: missile launches, launch time, launch a novel (publish), launch a server? Not exactly.
2. **"Press"**: press button, press cap (bottle cap?), press time? Not really.
3. **"Top"**: cap (top of head), top time (best time), top missile? Not great.
4. **"Missile" and "spy"**: both can be "intelligence" related? Missile is weapon, spy gathers intelligence.
5. **"Straw" and "cap"**: both can be parts of a bottle? Cap and straw.
6. **"Server" and "spy"**: both can be "agent"? Server agent, spy agent.
7. **"Novel" and "spy"**: spy novel!
8. **"Time" and "novel"**: "time novel"? Not really.

The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: missile, button, cap, time, novel, straw, server, himalayas, spy.
Your opponent's team words are: ice, car, shot, piano, lion, bank, fork, strike.
Distractor words are: beach, gold, bridge, whip, pumpkin, change, bill.
The kill words are: space.

The word cap was guessed correctly. The word bill was guessed but is an innocent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Remaining words for your opponent are: ice, car, shot, piano, lion, bank, fork, strike.
Remaining distractor words are: beach, gold, bridge, whip, pumpkin, change.
Remaining kill words are: space.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: missile, button, cap, time, novel, straw, server, himalayas, spy.
Your opponent's team words are: ice, car, shot, piano, lion, bank, fork, strike.
Distractor words are: beach, gold, bridge, whip, pumpkin, change, bill.
The kill words are: space.
